evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC for 4 classifiers with AUROC/AP in legend, diagonal chance line, and prevalence baseline — matches brief exactly.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set; PDF embeds DejaVuSans as Type 3 (not selectable); SVG has 0 text elements (all paths); default DejaVu Sans font used throughout.
evidence.palette: Colors (#4477AA, #EE6677, #228833, #888888) are Tol-like and colorblind-safe, but green (#228833) is assigned to 'OursModel' implying directional (good/ours) meaning rather than purely categorical.
evidence.layout_density: 8.5x3.8in figure, tight_layout(pad=1.5), axis ranges [-0.02,1.02]/[-0.02,1.05] are sensible; no gaping whitespace or overlapping labels observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and reference lines; legend placement (lower-right ROC, upper-right PRC) is appropriate; however caption states 'highest AUROC and AP' without giving the actual values.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is genuine vector (25KB, matplotlib PDF backend) but uses Type 3 fonts because pdf.fonttype=42 is not set — text is not selectable or editable in Illustrator/Inkscape.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (25KB), figure.png (80KB), figure.svg (75KB), caption.tex (417B), and gen_figure.py (2.4KB) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains both panels, names reference lines, and identifies OursModel as best performer, but omits the actual AUROC/AP numbers for OursModel in the caption body (only in legend).

overall_strengths: Scientific content is complete and matches the brief: correct curve types, all four methods, both reference lines, and AUROC/AP annotations in the legend.
All required output files are present and non-trivial; layout is clean with sensible axis ranges and no overlapping elements.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype) means text is rendered as Type 3 outlines/paths — not selectable or editable in vector editors, a standard journal requirement.

Caption lacks specific numerical values for the claimed best performer (OursModel's AUROC and AP), and green is used for 'OursModel' which conflates categorical identity with directional (good) meaning.
